rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 13: "In Cowork, you create one by going to *Customize* → *Skills* → *New* → *Create with Claude*. In Claude Code, the same shape is a `SKILL.md` under `~/.claude/skills/<name>/`."
rules_evaluated.fix: Move runtime-specific UI details to maintainer block; teaching stays to the concept (personal skills are author-able in both runtimes). Remove the "same shape" equivalence framing that defensively compares runtimes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 18: "**The check carries the expertise you fed it, and you author it.**" — structural framing with "compounding" concept not yet operationally earned in student-facing body at prework stage.
rules_evaluated.fix: Replace "compounding" framing with plain operational language: "The check carries the expertise you fed it; you write the logic." Or move the structural concept-framing to maintainer notes until M2 when compounding is earned.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line 15: "Not a separate model. Not magic. Not something you install per-seat."
rules_evaluated.fix: Cut the reassurance. Replace with operational statement: "You author one in your runtime, and it loads when the same kind of work appears again." The structural framing (personal + reusable) carries the work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 13: runtime-equivalence framing (defending Cowork parity with Claude Code) is positioning/marketing-shaped, not teaching-shaped. Reads as answering an objection (are these runtimes equivalent?) rather than teaching what skills are.
rules_evaluated.fix: Move UI paths and runtime comparison to maintainer notes or training-architecture docs. Body teaches the concept (personal skills = author-able, reusable expertise); maintainer context specifies where/how per runtime.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 29: "Non-deterministic behaviour. The same prompt, the same files, the same tools can produce different outputs on different runs." — term lands cold; context explains but does not earn operationally for engineer at prework stage.
rules_evaluated.fix: Earn in one breath before the abstract word. Replace: "The agent can produce different outputs from the same input — same prompt, same files, same tools, different results on different runs. This variability (non-deterministic behaviour) means a test that passed yesterday says nothing about today."

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9-10: "skill" disambiguated (SKILL.md + supporting files; reusable expertise).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose

rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework reading; mood-contract is taught in the Module 4 lecture, not the prework. Exemption applies.

summary: **Maintainer cut:** line 54. **Fence ranges:** none. **Verdict:** REVISE (3 blocking). Violations: (1) value-prop positioning leak at line 13 (runtime equivalence framing reads marketing-shaped, not teaching); (2) jargon planted too early at line 18 (structural framing before operational earning); (3) unearned technical term at line 29 (non-deterministic lands cold). One non-blocking over-hedge at line 15 (reassurance phrasing). File path: `/Users/anttitevanlinna/Projects/agents-102/curriculum/lectures/module-4-prework.md`
